title: DriftNarrative
description: Structured wrapper around a human-readable DriftReport rendering, produced by bin/explain-diff.sh when --format json is requested. The default markdown output is also derivable from this shape, so downstream consumers (chat bots, dashboards, custom PR templates) can re-render with their own copy. action_items[] mirrors the bullets the markdown narrative would suggest at the foot of the report so a consumer can build a checklist UI without re-parsing the prose.

target.description: Display name for the target repo. Pulled from the source DriftReport.target field; may be a basename or path depending on how the producer was invoked.

scope_applied.description: Mirrors DriftReport.scope_applied so consumers can render a 'partial scope' header. Omitted when the report covered the canonical 7-category set.

oneOf.description: Score not supplied to explain-diff (the consumer must skip the score-line in their rendering).
Signed delta from the previous session. Positive = improvement, negative = regression. null when no previous health snapshot exists.

trend_direction.description: Derived from trend_delta when available; 'unknown' when no prior session exists.

sections.description: One entry per drift category that had at least one finding. Order is significant: rendered in the same order in the markdown output, with critical/high severity floating to the top.

category.description: Drift sub-category emitted by explain-diff. Maps 1:1 to the source DriftReport block: `missing`/`misconfigured` from the top-level arrays, `claude_md`/`links`/`orphans`/`staleness` from `documentation.*`, `misplaced` from the misplaced-docs array, `history` from non_compliant_history.offenders, `documentation` reserved for subsystem-error reporting. Wider categories like `hooks` / `gitignore` aren't emitted because compute-drift already folds them into the missing/misconfigured arrays — explain-diff stays one-to-one with the source shape rather than re-classifying.

severity.description: Lead-phrase severity. Maps to: critical='Action required', high='Worth fixing', medium='Drifted', low='Minor', info=suppressed in the markdown render but kept in JSON for downstream filtering.

summary.description: One-line plain-English summary of this section. Suitable as a bullet in a digest.

items.description: Concrete per-finding lines (already formatted for display, not the raw JSON entry from the source DriftReport).

action_items.description: Ordered list of next steps the operator can take. Each item is a single imperative sentence (e.g. 'Run nyann:retrofit to fix the missing hooks').
